resolutions.action: Replaced 'Extreme Programming Explained' (1999) as the primary TDD reference with 'Test-Driven Development: By Example' (Beck, 2002), retaining the 1999 XP book as historical context.
Updated the Executive Summary to use '40–90%' to match the Nagappan et al. figure cited in Section 3 and the evidence table, eliminating the inconsistency.
Added a bullet point to the Limitations section acknowledging publication bias and the potential underrepresentation of null or negative results in the TDD literature.
Replaced the abbreviated inline source line with full bibliographic entries including authors, year, full title, journal/publisher, volume, issue, page range, and DOI where available.

Clarified that '18% more passing test cases' refers to externally defined black-box acceptance tests, not simply a greater number of developer-written tests, and supplemented with the study's additional finding on code quality.
Added concrete quantified findings from Madeyski (2010), specifically effect sizes for branch coverage and fault detection, to give the citation equal evidentiary weight to the other two studies.
